readings.title: Measuring the Gap Between FPGAs and ASICs
readings.note: The foundational study from the University of Toronto quantifying the area, speed, and power gap between FPGAs and ASICs. Measures the 20-35x area overhead of programmability and analyzes where the overhead comes from (routing dominates). Essential reading for understanding why FPGAs trade area for flexibility.

readings.title: AMD Versal Architecture and Product Data Sheet (DS950)
readings.note: Complete specifications for the Versal Adaptive SoC family, including the VC1902 (899,840 LUTs, 400 AI Engine tiles) and VP1902 (8.46M LUTs, 6,864 DSPs). Covers the heterogeneous architecture: programmable logic, AI Engines, DSP engines, hard processors, and Network-on-Chip.

readings.title: Intel Agilex 7 FPGA F-Series Device Data Sheet
readings.note: Specifications for the Agilex 7 family including the AGF027 (2.69M logic elements, 8,528 DSPs, 287 Mb embedded memory). Covers HyperFlex architecture, hardened floating-point support, and high-speed transceivers.

readings.title: Vitis High-Level Synthesis User Guide (UG1399)
readings.note: The definitive reference for HLS pragmas and design methodology. Covers pipeline, unroll, array_partition, dataflow, and interface directives with detailed examples. Essential for understanding how C++ maps to hardware.

readings.title: Build Fast, Trade Fast: FPGA-Based High-Frequency Trading Using High-Level Synthesis
readings.authors: Andrew Boutros, Abdelrahman Eldamaty, Muhsen Owaida, et al.
readings.note: Demonstrates a complete HFT system (network stack, FAST parser, order book, strategy, order gateway) implemented entirely in HLS achieving 270ns average tick-to-trade latency. Proves HLS viability for latency-critical trading with <8% logic utilization on Kintex UltraScale.

readings.title: ChipVerify -- Verilog Synthesis Patterns
readings.note: Practical reference for which Verilog constructs synthesize to which hardware. Covers the distinction between synthesizable (always blocks, assign, case) and simulation-only (initial, delays, system tasks) constructs.

readings.title: How Are FPGAs Used in Trading?
readings.note: First-person account from one of the world's largest market-making firms on why they build FPGA trading infrastructure in-house. Discusses the CPU vs FPGA vs ASIC trade-off from a practitioner's perspective and the importance of hardware-software co-design.
